name: CF Industries Holdings
summary: Created 2026-05-12 in response to **+8.2% 1D move on May 12 = MOVE OF THE DAY** in markets dossier. CF Industries is the world's largest publicly-traded nitrogen-fertilizer producer (urea, ammonia, UAN, AN); Q1 2026: net earnings $615M, EBITDA $983M; 99% available ammonia capacity utilization. **CRISIS LINKAGE: Qatar Ras Laffan offline since Feb 28 (QAFCO + SABIC Agri-Nutrients curtailed) = 35-40% global urea export capacity affected; urea US Gulf FOB futures +50% since war began; CF stock now +71% YTD.** UBS raised CF target $97→$140 May 12 noting target 'may not fully reflect severity of ongoing industry disruptions.' India IPL DAP tender May 11 received offers $935/t cfr east coast / $930/t cfr west coast = extraordinary (normal $580-650). PM Modi May 10-11 Hyderabad address asked Indians to cut fertilizer use ~50% = Indian-government public confirmation of binding shock, NOT hypothetical. CF +8.2% 1D = SUPPLY-DISRUPTION + DEMAND-CONFIRMATION confluence for first time in cycle. **Reversibility LOW: Ras Laffan not coming back inside 60-day window even under ceasefire; QAFCO scarring structural; India's import bill binding through monsoon planting season (June-Aug).** Cleanest crisis-linked long if Brent stays $100+ through Q3.

current.fundamentals: Q1 2026 net earnings $615M; EBITDA $983M; 99% available ammonia capacity utilization; UBS PT $97→$140 May 12

active_triggers.condition: CF Industries shares break $140 (UBS new PT) within 30 days — confirms fertilizer regime shift durable
Qatar Ras Laffan QAFCO + SABIC Agri-Nutrients resume normal exports within 60 days — reverses CF rally

recent_signals.headline: CF +8.2% 1D TO $124.48 = MOVE OF THE DAY. Drivers: Modi May 10-11 'cut fertilizer 50%' appeal = Indian-government public confirmation binding shock; Qatar Ras Laffan offline (QAFCO + SABIC Agri-Nutrients curtailed) = ~35-40% global urea export capacity affected; IPL DAP tender May 11 offers $935/t cfr east / $930/t cfr west (normal $580-650); urea US Gulf FOB futures +50% since war began; UBS raised target $97→$140 noting target 'may not fully reflect severity of ongoing industry disruptions'; CF Q1 2026 net earnings $615M / EBITDA $983M / 99% ammonia capacity utilization; YTD +71%. Sector regime shift: Nutrien +4.3%, BASF +3.5%, Linde +2.3% all bid together = not single-stock event. Secondary: Wheat +3.2%/Corn +3.2% same session = fertilizer cost pass-through.
sources: Argus + Indian fertilizer trade (DAP $935/t IPL tender)
Discovery Alert / Barchart (urea US Gulf futures +50%)
